---
title: "Human Services Focus"
subtitle: "The organizations that meet basic community needs — food, housing, employment, youth, safety"
---

::: {.key-finding}
<span class="kf-label">Key finding</span>
**771 active human services nonprofits** (NTEE groups I, J, K, L, O, P) serve
the region, generating about **$734 million a year** — led by Second Harvest
Inland Northwest, Goodwill Industries, and Catholic Charities of Spokane. The
mix skews heavily toward general human services and youth development;
**employment services (44 orgs) and housing & shelter (75 orgs) are the
thinnest categories** relative to need, and housing organizations are among
the region's smallest by revenue.
:::

{{< include _shared.qmd >}}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
orgs = FileAttachment("data/orgs_active.csv").csv({typed: true})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
formations = FileAttachment("data/formations.csv").csv({typed: true})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
countySummary = FileAttachment("data/county_summary.csv").csv({typed: true})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
hsLetters = new Map([
  ["I", "Crime & Legal-Related"],
  ["J", "Employment"],
  ["K", "Food, Agriculture & Nutrition"],
  ["L", "Housing & Shelter"],
  ["O", "Youth Development"],
  ["P", "Human Services (multipurpose)"]
])
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
hsOrgs = orgs.filter(d => hsLetters.has(d.ntee_major))
```

## What counts as "human services" here

This page drills into six NTEE major groups that correspond most directly to
basic community needs:

| NTEE | Category | Examples in the region |
|---|---|---|
| I | Crime & Legal-Related | legal aid, victim services, offender reentry |
| J | Employment | job training, vocational rehabilitation |
| K | Food, Agriculture & Nutrition | food banks, meal programs |
| L | Housing & Shelter | shelters, affordable-housing developers |
| O | Youth Development | mentoring, scouting, youth programs |
| P | Human Services | multipurpose agencies, family services, seniors |

## Organizations and dollars by category

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
hsByLetter = Array.from(hsLetters, ([letter, label]) => {
  const rows = hsOrgs.filter(d => d.ntee_major === letter);
  return {
    letter,
    label: `${label} (${letter})`,
    n: rows.length,
    revenue: d3.sum(rows, d => d.revenue > 0 ? d.revenue : 0)
  };
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 240,
  marginRight: 70,
  marginBottom: 45,
  height: 280,
  width: 900,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Active organizations", labelAnchor: "center", grid: true},
  y: {label: null},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(hsByLetter, {
      x: "n",
      y: "label",
      sort: {y: "-x"},
      fill: GU.s1,
      rx: 3,
      insetTop: 2,
      insetBottom: 2,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.label}\n${fnum(d.n)} organizations\nCombined revenue: ${fmoney(d.revenue)}`
    }),
    Plot.textX(hsByLetter, {
      x: "n", y: "label", text: d => fnum(d.n),
      dx: 6, textAnchor: "start", fill: GU.ink, fontSize: 12
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 240,
  marginRight: 80,
  marginBottom: 45,
  height: 280,
  width: 900,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Combined reported revenue ($)", labelAnchor: "center", grid: true, tickFormat: d => d3.format("~s")(d).replace("G", "B")},
  y: {label: null},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(hsByLetter, {
      x: "revenue",
      y: "label",
      sort: {y: "-x"},
      fill: GU.s3,
      rx: 3,
      insetTop: 2,
      insetBottom: 2,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.label}\n${fnum(d.n)} organizations\nCombined revenue: ${fmoney(d.revenue)}`
    }),
    Plot.textX(hsByLetter, {
      x: "revenue", y: "label", text: d => fmoney(d.revenue),
      dx: 6, textAnchor: "start", fill: GU.ink, fontSize: 12
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

::: {.chart-note}
Food & nutrition revenue is dominated by a single organization — Second
Harvest Inland Northwest ($152M), one of the largest food banks in the
Northwest. Youth development has the second-most organizations (151) but the
least combined revenue, a sign of a category built on small, volunteer-driven
groups.
:::

## Human services coverage by county

Organizations per 10,000 residents. Spokane County — the region's population
and need center — has the *lowest* per-capita human services coverage in the
region, a pattern examined in depth on the [Service Gaps](service-gaps.qmd)
page.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 130,
  marginRight: 70,
  marginBottom: 45,
  height: 300,
  width: 900,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Human services orgs per 10,000 residents", labelAnchor: "center", grid: true},
  y: {label: null},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(countySummary, {
      x: "hs_per_10k",
      y: "county",
      sort: {y: "-x"},
      fill: GU.s1,
      rx: 3,
      insetTop: 2,
      insetBottom: 2,
      tip: true,
      title: d => `${d.county}\n${fnum(d.n_human_services)} HS orgs / ${fnum(Math.round(d.population))} residents\n${d3.format(".1f")(d.hs_per_10k)} per 10k`
    }),
    Plot.textX(countySummary, {
      x: "hs_per_10k", y: "county", text: d => d3.format(".1f")(d.hs_per_10k),
      dx: 6, textAnchor: "start", fill: GU.ink, fontSize: 12
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"})
  ]
})
```

## Formation trend: is capacity keeping up?

New human-services organizations formed per year across the region (IRS
ruling dates, all vintages including since-dissolved organizations).

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| output: false
hsHist = {
  // formations.csv is aggregated across all categories, so recompute from the
  // active-org file for the human services subset (active orgs only).
  const rows = orgs.filter(d => hsLetters.has(d.ntee_major) && d.ruling_year >= 1970 && d.ruling_year <= 2025);
  const roll = d3.rollups(rows, v => v.length, d => d.ruling_year)
    .map(([year, n]) => ({year, n}))
    .sort((a, b) => a.year - b.year);
  for (let i = 0; i < roll.length; i++) {
    const w = roll.slice(Math.max(0, i - 4), i + 1);
    roll[i].ma5 = d3.mean(w, d => d.n);
  }
  return roll;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  height: 340,
  width: 900,
  marginLeft: 45,
  marginBottom: 45,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Year of IRS ruling", labelAnchor: "center", tickFormat: "d"},
  y: {label: "New HS organizations (still active)", grid: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.lineY(hsHist, {x: "year", y: "n", stroke: GU.s1, strokeWidth: 1.5, curve: "monotone-x"}),
    Plot.lineY(hsHist, {x: "year", y: "ma5", stroke: GU.red, strokeWidth: 2.5, curve: "monotone-x"}),
    Plot.ruleY([0], {stroke: "#c3c2b7"}),
    Plot.tip(hsHist, Plot.pointerX({
      x: "year", y: "n",
      title: d => `${d.year}\n${fnum(d.n)} new HS orgs\n5-yr average: ${d3.format(".1f")(d.ma5)}`
    }))
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
md`<span style="color:${GU.muted}; font-size:0.85rem;"><span style="color:${GU.s1}">━</span> annual &nbsp;&nbsp;<span style="color:${GU.red}">━</span> 5-year moving average. Counts only organizations still active in 2026, so early years understate true formation activity.</span>`
```

## Directory: every human services organization

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof hsCat = Inputs.select(
  ["All six categories"].concat(Array.from(hsLetters.values())),
  {label: "Category"}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof hsCounty = Inputs.select(
  ["All counties"].concat(Array.from(new Set(hsOrgs.map(d => d.county))).sort()),
  {label: "County"}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof hsSearch = Inputs.search(
  hsOrgs.filter(d =>
    (hsCat === "All six categories" || hsLetters.get(d.ntee_major) === hsCat) &&
    (hsCounty === "All counties" || d.county === hsCounty)
  ),
  {placeholder: "Search names, cities…", columns: ["name", "city"]}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(
  hsSearch.map(d => ({
    Name: d.name,
    City: d.city,
    County: d.county,
    Category: hsLetters.get(d.ntee_major),
    NTEE: d.ntee ?? "—",
    "Reported revenue": d.revenue > 0 ? fmoney(d.revenue) : "—"
  })),
  {rows: 15, layout: "auto"}
)
```

::: {.chart-note}
Source: NCCS Business Master File, June 2026 vintage. Revenue is the most
recent figure reported to the IRS.
:::
